recentTweets.text:
My prayers are with the family of Elmer Karl after his passing. Elmer was a true gentleman who treated every customer like a friend. The impact he made on his hometown of Gregory will live on as a legacy of his love for his community and for South Dakota.
 
I know there are many

Good to be in Rapid City this past weekend for the Black Hills Military Advisory Coalition’s 39th Annual Military Appreciation BBQ! The BHMAC does tremendous work for the community to support our military and their families. Grateful to be part of this celebration to thank this https://t.co/av4uKJfgOj
Met with the South Dakota FFA State Officers earlier today. It’s great to see such passion for agriculture from these young leaders. They are the future of the industry! https://t.co/ICzZJzk7NC

Big thank you to my team’s South Dakota interns, Josh and Cece in the Sioux Falls office and Megan in the Rapid City office! We appreciate your hard work this summer and wish you luck in your future endeavors. https://t.co/R37qVuxQK8

Jay Clayton is a true professional who is prepared to serve our country as the next Director of National Intelligence. I’m honored that he asked me to introduce him at today’s hearing and look forward to working with him once confirmed. https://t.co/5Q6bRmjHnR

These are just four of numerous provisions in this legislation. The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act passed the Senate and the House with broad bipartisan support. This is good legislation that will have a real, positive effect on families in South Dakota and across the nation. A

The Better Use of Intergovernmental & Local Development (BUILD) Housing Act is also aimed at modernizing NEPA by increasing capacity sharing between HUD and state and local governments. Better flow of information regarding environmental reviews between these entities cuts red

The Unlocking Housing Supply Through Streamlined and Modernized Review Act streamlines the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) review process for U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) funded projects making it faster to build new affordable homes. This

Today the 21st Century ROAD to Housing becomes law, marking the most comprehensive housing legislation in decades. This legislation has provisions that take real strides toward cutting red tape and modernizing existing programs, making the American dream of homeownership a

The Rural Housing Service Reform Act is the most significant update to the Rural Housing Service in decades. One key piece of this legislation is an update to the U.S. Department of Agriculture’s Section 515 program to decouple rental assistance from maturing mortgages. This

250 years ago today, our Founding Fathers began our nation with the blessings of the Almighty. The United States of America continues to be the greatest nation in the world.  Wishing all South Dakotans a happy, safe and freedom-filled Independence Day. May the good Lord continue https://t.co/XRrcZe6OLR
Great to join @BretBaier live from Mount Rushmore ahead of tonight’s ceremony. Can’t think of a better way to celebrate 250 years of our nation than under the great faces! https://t.co/sxnNRAHb6e

Today marks one year since Senate Republicans passed the Working Families Tax Cuts Act, the largest tax relief package for the working class in history.

Thanks to the WFTC, the average American family received a $3,750 tax cut and saw an 11% increase in their tax refund. In

As long as I am in the United States Senate, we will not be a society where woke Democrats have a say in your child’s gender identity or where girls are forced to compete against boys in sports. Pleased to see the Supreme Court of the United States ruled that States are allowed
Had a good time meeting students from Harrisburg North Middle School during their tour of our nation’s capital last week. They had lots of thoughtful questions for me! https://t.co/P6Zc1oqkgh

My office continues to hear from South Dakotans on a daily basis about the issues they’re experiencing with mail delivery. At my request, the Office of the Inspector General has opened an official investigation into South Dakota’s postal service.

We have heard your concerns and

In Highmore today as first responders, utility crews and local officials work to keep the community safe and begin the recovery process. Thanking them for their dedication and commitment during a very difficult time for their community. https://t.co/aBONCu68Os

Enjoyed speaking with students visiting DC with the South Dakota Rural Electric Association Youth Tour. This trip is a great opportunity for students to learn how electric cooperatives play an important role in strengthening rural communities. https://t.co/QfvGHI6zeo

Sat down with Joshua from Sioux Falls and Jonathan from Watertown representing the American Academy of Family Physicians. We had a good conversation about ways to strengthen access to primary care in South Dakota. https://t.co/XlbMz8Os1w

Mail service in South Dakota has continued to deteriorate, all the while the Postmaster General insists there’s no problem. As a result, I recently announced my intent to place a blanket hold on all nominations to the Board of Governors of the United States Postal Service. I will https://t.co/u8tGhIDl39

Two of the very best in South Dakota agriculture. Jerry Schmitz and Glenn Muller have been my friends for years. I’ve appreciated the opportunity to work alongside them as they tirelessly advocated for South Dakota farmers, ranchers and rural communities. Their leadership has https://t.co/3t3NQQrMwD

I’m looking forward to attending the fireworks show at Mount Rushmore on July 3 with President Trump. In South Dakota, we don’t just climb mountains – we carve them. 

Thanks to President Trump and his team, fireworks will once again be displayed over Mount Rushmore for the first

Enjoyed visiting with students from across South Dakota who are in town competing at the National History Day contest. Proud to see them representing South Dakota at the national level! https://t.co/bUwxVkNC5j
Visited with Liz Olive, CEO of Vista Care South Dakota based in Pierre. Important to hear directly about the challenges facing South Dakotans who rely on disability services. https://t.co/znYpX2roU7

Enjoyed visiting Mitchell for the Department of South Dakota Veterans of Foreign Wars 96th Annual State Convention. It was great to join VFW members, Auxiliary members and veteran advocates from across the state and thank our veterans for their service and sacrifice on behalf of https://t.co/8fXQbPh9sV
https://t.co/q3b30SxNSU

Artificial intelligence is here to stay, and America has an opportunity to lead. Grateful for the chance to speak at the USA AI Summit about the role we play in creating a regulatory environment that drives domestic development in a positive direction. https://t.co/G2ji6XoOOy

Nice to meet with with Rachel, who serves as the President of Bridgewater Emery FFA. I enjoyed hearing about her interest and experience in the agriculture industry. Good to see young South Dakotans interested in pursuing careers in agriculture. https://t.co/QvS5pdzBEj

Enjoyed meeting with Brandon and Madisen from SoDak Diesel & Performance LLC in Pierre. We discussed challenges small businesses are facing today and ways we can continue to support their growth in South Dakota. https://t.co/uWFL71kPHP

We had a group of South Dakotans with the National Association of Home Builders in DC to talk about the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act and ways we can support our local communities. https://t.co/Lj5KmC2oCp

Great to see students from Deuel County today on their visit to D.C. They are enjoying their visit to our nation’s capital and told me their favorite part so far has been the monuments! https://t.co/SEPGyPaI82

Good meeting with Jay Clayton this evening, who has been nominated by the President to serve as the next Director of National Intelligence (DNI). I first met Jay in my role on the Banking committee when he was going through his confirmation to serve as SEC chairman. I believe https://t.co/QDYQDiJaOl
Sat down with South Dakota Health Care Association members from across South Dakota to hear their perspective on current health care workforce challenges. https://t.co/h4nPKA2rDY

Good to sit down with Rabbi Mendel Alperowitz who established the Chabad Jewish Center of South Dakota. He is currently the only rabbi in South Dakota, and we appreciate all he does for our Jewish community. https://t.co/5sfED4BYnt

Lisa Jennings and Dawn Marie Johnson with the Sioux Falls School District stopped by our DC office to talk about the future of public education. Appreciate all they do for the younger generation of South Dakotans! https://t.co/c4T9RuTKwr
I sat down with city leadership in Milbank to discuss a number of projects they’re working on, including improvements in their water system. https://t.co/I0Sbkn5Cpd
Enjoyed visiting Milbank for Cheeseburger Day! Valley Queen Cheese has put on this event for the community for 35 years. Nice to see so many familiar faces. https://t.co/rn2WKJvJ84
On the anniversary of D-Day, we remember the courage, sacrifice and heroism of the men and women in uniform who helped change the course of history. https://t.co/avN5rNJ7tV

Always enjoy visiting with South Dakota farmers and ranchers. I had an informative conversation with Trevor Johnson from Centerville about the future of agriculture and the opportunities available for young people looking to make their living in farming and ranching. https://t.co/tgveMYVoiw

I recently got to meet South Dakota State students participating in the NASA Gateways to Blue Skies Challenge. Out of just eight teams nationally invited to the finals, three teams came from SDSU and one team brought home the national championship!

Congratulations to these https://t.co/euWrbfQhVB

This morning, the Senate is beginning to vote on a reconciliation bill to continue funding for ICE and border patrol. While we wish this could have been funded through regular order appropriations, our Democrat colleagues have refused to fund these agencies. This bill will fund

Visited Boys State in Aberdeen today to talk with young men participating from across the state. Appreciated their insightful questions about current events and my work in the Senate. Have a great week! https://t.co/DX4qRayWj0

Had a productive roundtable conversation with small business leaders from the Aberdeen area, where we discussed everything from cybersecurity to beef imports and exports. Thank you to Reede Construction for hosting! https://t.co/vvsPAs8mRY

Enjoyed visiting Vermillion this morning to talk with Girls State delegates from across South Dakota. They had lots of thoughtful questions about public service. The future is bright! https://t.co/nF6Z1LwPEf

The Flandreau High School Marching Band marched through the streets of DC this morning in the National Memorial Day Parade. Pleased to have nominated them for this honor. Congratulations to the students and their director Patrick Weight on a great performance! https://t.co/bxh4GgGO6d

On Memorial Day, we pause and remember our men and women in uniform who gave everything for our nation. We honor their sacrifice, courage and the freedoms they fought to protect. Home of the free, because of the brave. https://t.co/4E2KLsxVJg

Sat down with Greg Wick, President of Pennington Title Company in Rapid City and Travis Martin from Black Hills Title in Belle Fourche. Good to talk to them about ways to protect homebuyers and help minimize the risk of real estate fraud. https://t.co/FaQXMsKmPq

Had a good conversation with South Dakota doctors representing American College of Physicians about the work they’re doing as well as the role artificial intelligence will play in diagnosing and curing diseases in the very near future. https://t.co/5gWjWO8w9b

Sat down with South Dakotans from the American Association of People with Disabilities. Grateful they took the time to share their personal stories and talk about ways Congress can help support those with disabilities. https://t.co/S0HBD8OjpB

Great to see Peter Mehlhaff, CEO and Chairman of Great Plains Bank and Director of the Federal Home Loan Bank of Des Moines. We discussed banking, housing and the Federal Home Loan Bank. https://t.co/zXW11QNVsn
Sat down with Matt Campbell with My Place Hotels of America, good to talk with him about the latest in the hospitality industry. https://t.co/0Cy46j525w
Good to see Doug Curry and Rob Timm with the City of Box Elder to get an update on the projects they are working on as their community prepares for the arrival of the B-21. https://t.co/xpG4UHQqw0

The Senate has confirmed Dan Satterlee to serve as U.S. Marshal for South Dakota. Dan has dedicated his career to serving South Dakotans and I’m confident he’ll continue that strong leadership in this new role.

I recently met with members of the Freeman Mennonites in Sioux Falls, SD. It was good to see them, hear their concerns and provide them an update on the work going on in Washington, D.C. https://t.co/qTlJCoOiAY

Great news from the House today with the passage of legislation authorizing the year-round sale of E15. This a win for farmers and ranchers AND a win for consumers who would see lower fuel prices at the pump. I look forward to supporting this legislation when it reaches the

Last Friday, I joined Southeast Technical College for the groundbreaking ceremony of its new Wells Fargo Homebuilding Lab. This facility will offer students studying to work in construction fields the opportunity to work year-round in a covered facility on homes that will later https://t.co/PQnSj5eTqm

Access to affordable housing is essential to strengthening our communities and securing the future of South Dakota. Earlier this year, the Senate passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act which includes several of my bills to cut unnecessary red tape that slows housing

Great to be at the Sanford Underground Research Facility in Lead yesterday to begin the next phase of construction for the Long-Baseline Neutrino Facility and Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment (LBNF/DUNE). 

@sanfordlab is the result of many people who had a vision to develop https://t.co/XP45GtNJVK
